title: Beef Brisket Pot Roast
instructions: Score the fat side of the brisket and salt it. Brown both sides in a Dutch oven. Remove, then sauté onions and garlic in the rendered fat. Tie herbs together. Return brisket to pot, add herbs and beef stock, bring to a simmer, then bake at 300°F (150°C) for 3 hours, flipping hourly. Add carrots and cook for another hour. Remove brisket and rest. Optional: blend pan juices into a sauce with mustard. Slice meat against the grain and serve with carrots, onions, and mashed potatoes.

title: Chicken & Halloumi Burgers
instructions: Flatten chicken breasts to 1cm thickness using parchment and a rolling pin. Cut each into two. If pan-frying, heat two pans; cook chicken in one with oil for 3–4 mins per side. Add chilli sauce and half the lemon juice; cook another 1–2 mins. For air-fryer method, cook chicken at 180°C for 12 mins, add chilli sauce and lemon juice, cook another 1–2 mins. Toast buns in dry pan for 30 secs or air-fry for 1–2 mins. Increase air fryer to 200°C, cook halloumi for 10 mins, flipping halfway. Toss cabbage with mayo and remaining lemon juice. Assemble burgers: hummus on buns, then rocket, chilli chicken, halloumi, peppers, drizzle of sauce, cabbage, and bun tops. Serve extra cabbage or salad on the side.

title: Chocolate Raspberry Brownies
instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4. Line a 20 x 30cm baking tray with parchment. 2. In a pan, melt dark chocolate, milk chocolate, butter, and sugar, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat. 3. Stir in eggs, one at a time, into the melted mixture. 4. Sieve over flour and cocoa, then stir in. 5. Add half of the raspberries and stir. Pour mixture into the tray, and scatter the remaining raspberries on top. 6. Bake for 30 minutes or 35 minutes for a firmer texture. 7. Cool before slicing into squares. 8. Store in an airtight container for up to 3 days.

title: Krispy Kreme Donut
instructions: 1. Dissolve yeast in warm water in a 2 1/2-quart bowl. 2. Add milk, sugar, salt, eggs, shortening, and 2 cups flour. Beat on low for 30 seconds, scraping the bowl constantly. Beat on medium speed for 2 minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally. 3. Stir in the remaining flour until smooth. Cover and let rise until double, 50-60 minutes. 4. Turn dough onto a floured surface and roll it lightly in flour. Roll dough to 1/2-inch thickness with a floured rolling pin. 5. Cut dough with a floured doughnut cutter. Cover and let rise for 30-40 minutes. 6. Heat vegetable oil in a deep fryer to 350°. Slide doughnuts into the hot oil. Turn doughnuts as they rise to the surface. Fry until golden brown, about 1 minute on each side. 7. Remove carefully from oil, drain, and dip into the creamy glaze. For glaze: 8. Heat butter until melted, then stir in powdered sugar and vanilla until smooth. Stir in water, 1 tablespoon at a time, until desired consistency.

title: Apple Frangipane Tart
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200C/180C Fan/Gas 6. Crush the digestive biscuits into fine crumbs and mix with melted butter. Press into a tart tin and chill. Cream butter and sugar until fluffy, mix in eggs, ground almonds, and almond extract. Arrange sliced apples over the biscuit base and spread the frangipane filling on top. Sprinkle flaked almonds over. Bake for 20–25 minutes until golden and set. Cool before removing from the tin. Serve warm with cream, crème fraîche, or ice cream.

title: Broccoli & Stilton soup
instructions: Heat the rapeseed oil in a large saucepan and then add the onions. Cook on a medium heat until soft. Add a splash of water if the onions start to catch. Add the celery, leek, potato and a knob of butter. Stir until melted, then cover with a lid. Allow to sweat for 5 minutes. Remove the lid. Pour in the stock and add any chunky bits of broccoli stalk. Cook for 10 – 15 minutes until all the vegetables are soft. Add the rest of the broccoli and cook for a further 5 minutes. Carefully transfer to a blender and blitz until smooth. Stir in the stilton, allowing a few lumps to remain. Season with black pepper and serve.

title: Apple & Blackberry Crumble
instructions: Preheat oven to 190C/170C fan/gas 5. Combine flour and sugar, then rub in butter to make breadcrumbs. Bake on a tray for 15 minutes. Meanwhile, cook apples in butter and sugar to caramelize, then add blackberries and cinnamon. Let sit covered for 2–3 minutes. Transfer fruit to an ovenproof dish, top with crumble mix, and bake for another 5–10 minutes. Serve with vanilla ice cream.

title: Bakewell Tart
instructions: Make pastry by rubbing butter into flour until it resembles breadcrumbs. Add water and form a dough. Roll out and line a 20cm flan tin. Chill for 30 mins. Preheat oven to 200C/400F/Gas 6. Bake blind for 15 mins, then another 5 mins without beans. Spread jam on base. Melt butter, mix in sugar, ground almonds, egg, and almond extract. Pour mixture into tart and sprinkle with flaked almonds. Bake for 35 mins, covering loosely with foil if almonds brown too fast.

title: Bread and Butter Pudding
instructions: Grease a pie dish with butter. Cut crusts off bread, butter slices, and cut into triangles. Layer bread and sultanas in dish, sprinkling with cinnamon. Warm milk and cream to scalding, don’t boil. Whisk eggs with ¾ sugar, add warm milk, stir, and strain. Pour over bread. Sprinkle nutmeg and remaining sugar. Let stand 30 mins. Bake at 180C/355F/Gas 4 for 30–40 mins until set and golden.

title: Beef Wellington
instructions: Process mushrooms with seasoning into a paste, cook until moisture is gone. Cool. Sear beef fillet briefly and coat with mustard. Spread mushroom paste over Parma ham slices on cling film, place beef in center and roll tightly. Chill 15–20 mins. Roll out pastry, place beef in center, brush with egg yolk, and wrap. Chill again. Score pastry and glaze with egg yolk. Bake at 200C/400F for 20 mins, then at 180C/350F for 15 mins. Rest 10–15 mins before slicing.

title: Beef Sunday Roast
instructions: Boil broccoli and carrots until tender. Roast beef and potatoes in oven for 45 minutes. For Yorkshire puddings, preheat oven to 230°C/210°C fan. Add oil to muffin tin and heat. Mix 140g flour, 4 eggs, and 200ml milk to form a lump-free batter. Season, pour into hot tin, and bake for 20–25 minutes until puffed. Serve roast with veggies, Yorkshire puddings, and gravy.

title: Baingan Bharta
instructions: Roast the eggplant over an open flame until soft and smoky, optionally inserting garlic cloves before roasting. Cool in water, peel, and chop or mash. Heat oil in a pan, sauté chopped onions and garlic until translucent, then add green chilies and tomatoes. Cook until oil separates. Add red chili powder, then the mashed eggplant. Mix well and cook 4–5 minutes. Season with salt and garnish with coriander leaves. Serve with roti or rice.

title: Chicken Handi
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a large pot or wok. Once the oil is hot, fry sliced onions until deep golden brown. Remove and set aside. 2. In the same pot, sauté chopped garlic for a minute, then add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft (about 5 minutes). 3. Return the fried onions to the pot and stir. Add ginger paste and sauté well. 4. Add cumin seeds, half of the coriander seeds, and chopped green chillies. Stir quickly. 5. Add spices – turmeric powder and red chili powder. Sauté for a couple of minutes. 6. Add chicken pieces, season with salt, and cook covered on medium-low heat for about 15 minutes until the chicken is almost cooked. 7. Once oil separates from spices, add beaten yogurt on low heat to prevent splitting. 8. Sprinkle the remaining coriander seeds and half of the dried fenugreek leaves. Mix well. 9. Add cream, mix again, and sprinkle remaining kasuri methi and garam masala. Serve hot with naan or rotis.

title: Recheado Masala Fish
instructions: 1. Soak all the spices, ginger, garlic, tamarind pulp, and Kashmiri chilies (except oil) in vinegar. Add sugar, salt, and turmeric powder. Combine all nicely and marinate for 35-40 minutes. 2. Grind the mixture until soft and smooth. If required, add more vinegar, but ensure the paste remains thick to stick to the fish. 3. Rinse the fish and make incisions from the top and center. Stuff the paste into the fish and coat it entirely. Marinate for 30 minutes. 4. Heat oil in a shallow pan. Once hot, shallow fry the stuffed fish until golden brown on both sides. 5. Serve hot with salad, lime wedges, rice, and curry.

title: Tandoori Chicken
instructions: 1. Mix the lemon juice with paprika and red onions in a large shallow dish. Slash each chicken thigh three times, then turn them in the juice and set aside for 10 minutes. 2. Mix all of the marinade ingredients together and pour over the chicken. Mix everything well, then cover and chill for at least 1 hour. (This can be done up to a day in advance). 3. Heat the grill. Place the chicken pieces onto a rack over a baking tray. Brush with a little oil and grill for 8 minutes on each side, or until lightly charred and fully cooked through.

title: Lamb Biryani
instructions: 1. Grind the cashews, poppy seeds, and cumin seeds into a smooth paste, using as little water as possible. Set aside. 2. Deep fry the sliced onions in hot oil. Do not overcrowd the oil. Once the onions turn light brown, remove them from the oil and drain on a paper towel. 3. Fry the cashewnuts until golden brown and set aside. 4. Wash the rice and soak it in water for 20 minutes. 5. In a pan, heat oil and add sliced onions, blended paste, green chilies, ginger garlic paste, and garlic. Fry for a minute. 6. Add tomatoes and sauté until they are cooked (not mushy). 7. Add red chili powder, biryani powder, mint, coriander leaves, and sauté well. 8. Add yogurt and mix (move the skillet away from the heat to prevent curdling). 9. Add the washed lamb, salt, and ½ cup water. Cook for 1 hour on medium-low heat or cook in a pressure cooker for 6 whistles. 10. Once cooked, if water remains, heat the mixture uncovered to evaporate excess liquid. 11. In another large pan, boil rice in thrice the amount of water. Add salt and cumin seeds. Once the rice is 80% cooked (after 7 minutes), drain the rice. 12. Start layering: in the lamb mixture, add a layer of drained rice on top. Garnish with fried onions, ghee, mint, coriander leaves, and saffron dissolved in milk. 13. Cover the dish and bake at 350°F for 15 minutes or cook on medium heat for 12 minutes and lowest heat for 5 minutes. 14. Mix well before serving hot.
ingredients: ½ tsp Saffron (dissolved in ½ cup warm milk)

title: Cajun Spiced Fish Tacos
instructions: 1. Mix the cajun spice and cayenne pepper with a little seasoning and coat the fish all over. 2. Heat oil in a frying pan, add the fish, and cook over medium heat until golden. Reduce heat and fry until cooked through, about 10 minutes. 3. Prepare the dressing by combining all the dressing ingredients with a little seasoning. 4. Soften tortillas in the microwave for 5–10 seconds. 5. To assemble: pile tortillas with avocado, lettuce, spring onion, salsa, large flakes of fish, and drizzle with dressing.

title: Chicken Enchilada Casserole
instructions: 1. Cut each chicken breast into 3 pieces to speed up cooking. Place the chicken in a pot and pour Enchilada sauce over it. 2. Cook covered on low to medium heat for about 20 minutes, stirring occasionally. 3. Remove the chicken and shred with two forks. 4. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). 5. In a baking dish, spread ¼ cup of enchilada sauce on the bottom. 6. Layer as follows: 2 corn tortillas, ⅓ of the chicken, ⅓ of the remaining sauce, ⅓ of the cheese. Repeat for two more layers. 7. Bake uncovered for 20–30 minutes, until bubbly and the cheese is melted and golden brown. 8. Serve warm.

title: Stuffed Bell Peppers with Quinoa and Black Beans
instructions: 1. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Lightly grease a 9x13-inch baking dish. 2. Place the bell pepper halves in the prepared baking dish, cut side up. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until slightly softened. 3. While the peppers bake, heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook for 3-4 minutes. Add the garlic and cook for 1 more minute. 4. Stir in the quinoa, black beans, corn, diced tomatoes, cumin, chili powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook for 5-7 minutes until heated through. Stir in 1 cup of shredded cheese if desired. 5. Remove peppers from the oven and stuff with the quinoa mixture. Top with the remaining cheese. 6. Bake for another 15-20 minutes, until the cheese is melted and the peppers are tender. 7. Let the peppers cool for 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh cilantro.

title: Braised Beef Chilli
instructions: Preheat oven to 120°C (250°F). Sear beef in olive oil, remove, then brown chorizo. Sauté chopped onions, garlic, spices, herbs, and chillies in chorizo oil until soft. Season and add vinegar, tomatoes, ketchup, and sugar. Return meats to pot, add water or wine, bring to simmer, cover, and cook in oven for 2 hours. Add borlotti beans and cook for 1 hour more. Before serving, shred meat with forks.

title: Beef Stroganoff
instructions: Cook onions in olive oil until soft (15 mins), adding garlic partway. Add butter, then mushrooms, cook 5 mins. Season and set aside. Coat beef in seasoned flour and brown for 3–4 mins. Return onions and mushrooms to pan. Mix crème fraîche, mustard, and stock, pour in, and simmer 5 mins. Garnish with parsley. Serve with pappardelle or rice.

title: Fish Soup (Ukha)
instructions: 1. Heat the olive oil in a medium pot over medium-high heat. Add the onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to caramelize. 2. Add the carrots and cook until they start to soften, about 4 minutes. 3. Add the stock, water, potatoes, bay leaves, and black peppercorns. Season with salt and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat, cover, and cook for 10 minutes. 4. Add the millet and cook for an additional 15 minutes until the millet and potatoes are cooked. 5. Gently add the fish cubes. Stir and bring the soup to a simmer. The fish cooks quickly, so ensure not to overcook it. The fish is done when the flesh is opaque and flakes easily. 6. Garnish with chopped fresh dill or parsley before serving.

title: Lamb Pilaf (Plov)
instructions: 1. Place the raisins and prunes into a small bowl and pour over enough water to cover. Add lemon juice and let soak for at least 1 hour. Drain and roughly chop the prunes. 2. Heat butter in a large pan, add the onion, and cook for 5 minutes. 3. Add cubed lamb, ground lamb, and crushed garlic cloves. Fry for 5 minutes, stirring constantly until browned. 4. Pour 2/3 cup (150 milliliters) of stock into the pan. Bring to a boil, then lower the heat, cover, and simmer for 1 hour, or until the lamb is tender. 5. Add the remaining stock and bring to a boil. 6. Add rinsed long-grain white rice and a large pinch of saffron. Stir, then cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the rice is tender. 7. Add the drained raisins, chopped prunes, salt, and pepper to taste. Heat through for a few minutes. 8. Turn out onto a warmed serving dish and garnish with flat-leaf parsley sprigs.

title: Strawberries Romanoff
instructions: 1. In a medium bowl, combine hulled and quartered strawberries, 4 Tbsp sugar, and 4 Tbsp liqueur. Stir to combine, then cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour and up to 2 hours, stirring once or twice. 2. Just before serving, in a large mixing bowl, combine 1 cup cold heavy cream and 1/4 cup powdered sugar, and beat with an electric mixer until stiff peaks form. 3. Using a spatula, fold in 1/4 cup sour cream just until well blended. 4. Stir strawberries and divide them between 6 serving glasses or bowls. You can spoon a little syrup over the berries if you like. 5. Spoon the cream over the strawberries, dividing evenly. You can also use an ice cream scoop with a trigger release for a nice rounded puff of cream. Serve right away or chill and enjoy within 2 hours of assembly.

title: Apam Balik
instructions: Mix milk, oil, and eggs. Sift flour, baking powder, and salt into the mixture. Stir well. Pour batter into a pan and spread thinly. Cover for 30–60 seconds until bubbles appear. Add butter, cream corn, crushed peanuts, and sugar. Fold pancake in half and cook until browned. Cut into wedges and serve warm.

title: Ayam Percik
instructions: Blend shallots, ginger, garlic, and spices into a paste. Cook the paste for 10 mins, adding water/oil as needed. Add tamarind paste, coconut milk, sugar, salt, and water. Simmer for 10 mins. Cool and pour over chicken to marinate overnight. Roast chicken at 425°F for 35–45 mins until internal temp reaches 175°F. Rest before serving with sauce and rice.

title: Apam Balik
instructions: 1. Mix milk, oil, and egg together. 2. Sift flour, baking powder, and salt into the mixture. Stir well until all ingredients are combined evenly. 3. Spread some batter onto the pan, then spread a thin layer of batter to the side of the pan. Cover the pan for 30-60 seconds until small air bubbles appear. 4. Add butter, cream corn, crushed peanuts, and sugar onto the pancake. 5. Fold the pancake into half once the bottom surface is browned. 6. Cut into wedges and serve warm for the best taste.

title: Beef Lo Mein
instructions: STEP 1 - MARINATING THE BEEF: In a bowl, add the beef, salt, 1 pinch white pepper, 1 teaspoon sesame seed oil, 1/2 egg, corn starch, 1 tablespoon of oil and mix together. STEP 2 - BOILING THE NOODLES: In a 6 qt pot, add your noodles to boiling water until the noodles are submerged and boil on high heat for 10 seconds. After the noodles are done boiling, strain and cool with cold water. STEP 3 - STIR FRY: Add 2 tablespoons of oil to a wok, cook beef on high heat until medium cooked, then set the beef aside. In the same wok, add 2 tablespoons of oil, onions, minced garlic, minced ginger, bean sprouts, mushrooms, peapods, and 1.5 cups of water or until the vegetables are submerged in water. Add the noodles to the wok. To make the sauce, add oyster sauce, 1 pinch white pepper, 1 teaspoon sesame seed oil, sugar, and 1 teaspoon of soy sauce. Add the beef to the wok and stir-fry.

title: Chicken Congee
instructions: STEP 1 - MARINATING THE CHICKEN: In a bowl, add chicken, salt, white pepper, ginger juice and mix together well. Set the chicken aside. STEP 2 - RINSE THE WHITE RICE: Rinse the rice in a metal bowl or pot a couple of times and then drain the water. STEP 3 - BOILING THE WHITE RICE: Add 8 cups of water and set the stove on high heat until boiling. Once the rice porridge starts to boil, set the stove to low heat and stir it once every 8-10 minutes for about 20-25 minutes. After 25 minutes, you can add more water to adjust the consistency. Add the marinated chicken to the rice porridge and cook on low heat for another 10 minutes. After 10 minutes, add green onions, sliced ginger, a pinch of salt, and a pinch of white pepper. Stir for 10 seconds. Serve the rice porridge in a bowl. Optional: Garnish with coriander on top.

title: Hot and Sour Soup
instructions: STEP 1 - MAKING THE SOUP: In a wok, add chicken broth and wait for it to boil. Next, add salt, sugar, sesame seed oil, white pepper, hot pepper sauce, vinegar, and soy sauce, stirring for a few seconds. Add tofu, mushrooms, and black wood ear mushrooms to the wok. To thicken the sauce, whisk together 1 tablespoon of cornstarch and 2 tablespoons of water in a bowl, then slowly add it to the soup until it reaches the desired thickness. Next, add 1 slightly beaten egg and stir for 8 seconds. Serve the soup in a bowl, topping it with BBQ pork and sliced green onions.

title: Szechuan Beef
instructions: STEP 1 - MARINATING THE BEEF: In a bowl, add the beef, salt, sesame seed oil, white pepper, egg white, 2 tablespoons of corn starch, and 1 tablespoon of oil. STEP 2 - STIR FRY: First, cook the beef by adding 2 tablespoons of oil until the beef is golden brown. Set the beef aside. In a wok, add 1 tablespoon of oil, minced ginger, minced garlic, and stir-fry for a few seconds. Next, add all of the vegetables and then add sherry cooking wine and 1 cup of water. To make the sauce, add oyster sauce, hot pepper sauce, and sugar. Add the cooked beef and 1 tablespoon of soy sauce. To thicken the sauce, whisk together 1 tablespoon of cornstarch and 2 tablespoons of water in a bowl and slowly add to your stir-fry until it's the right thickness.
